latest.title: Block Listing Six Monthly Return and Cancellation
latest.content:
Today 09:44
RNS Number : 0944F
Videndum PLC
20 May 2026
20 May 2026
Videndum plc
BLOCK LISTING SIX MONTHLY RETURN AND CANCELLATION
Videndum plc (the "Company") announces the cancellation of certain historic block listings in respect of an aggregate 372,202 shares, previously admitted under the Videndum 2011 UK Sharesave Scheme, Videndum 2011 International Sharesave Plan, Videndum 2020 UK Sharesave Plan and Videndum 2020 International Sharesave Plan.
In connection with the Capital Raising and Capital Reorganisation announced on 10 March 2026, the Company's Existing Ordinary Shares of 20 pence nominal value were sub-divided and converted into 1 Intermediate Share of 0.005 pence nominal value and 1 Deferred Share of 19.995 pence. Immediately thereafter, every 200 Intermediate Shares of 0.005 pence nominal value were consolidated into 1 Consolidated Share of 1 pence nominal value under a new ISIN.
The block listings referred to above related to the ordinary shares of 20 pence nominal value and were not transferred to the new ISIN. Accordingly, the block listings have been cancelled, and no further shares will be allotted in respect of them.
The Company also confirms that it has made no allotments under these plans since the last block listing return was released on 19 November 2025.
A final block listing return is set out below in relation to the cancelled block listings.
Name of the company:
Videndum plc
Name of relevant scheme:
1. Videndum 2011 UK Sharesave Scheme
2. Videndum 2011 International Sharesave Plan
3. Videndum 2020 UK Sharesave Plan and;
4. Videndum 2020 International Sharesave Plan
Period of return:
From:
19 November 2025
To:
19 May 2026
Balance of unallotted securities under scheme(s) from previous return:
1. 2,279
2. 13,119
3. 159,892
4. 196,912
Total:
372,202 shares
Plus:
The amount by which the block scheme(s) has been increased since the date of the last return (if any increase has been applied for):
1. to 4. inclusive: Nil
Less:
Number of
securities
issued/allotted under scheme(s) during period:
1. to 4. inclusive: Nil
Equals:
Balance under scheme(s) not yet issued/allotted at end of period:
1. 2,279
2. 13,119
3. 159,892
4. 196,912
Total:
372,202 shares
This is the final return. No further shares will be allotted pursuant to this block listing.
Capitalised terms used in this announcement have the meanings given to them in the "Comprehensive Refinancing" announcement dated 10 March 2026, unless the context provides otherwise.
Enquiries:
Jon Bolton
Group Company Secretary
020 8332 4602
A snapshot of Videndum plc
Videndum is a leading global provider of premium branded hardware products and software solutions to the content creation market.
Our product portfolio includes camera supports, video transmission systems and monitors, live streaming solutions, robotic camera systems, prompters, LED lighting, mobile power, bags, backgrounds, audio capture and noise reduction equipment. We employ around 1,200 people across the world in 9 different countries. Videndum plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange, ticker: VID.
More information can be found at:
https://videndum.com/
LEI number: 2138007H5DQ4X8YOCF14

While the Directors are pleased that all resolutions at the AGM were supported by a significant majority of shareholders, the Board notes that more than 20 per cent of votes were cast against Resolutions 5 and 7 which received a vote of 72.65% respectively each in favour. The Board believes that the votes against relate to the composition of the Remuneration Committee and in line with the UK Corporate Governance Code, we intend to consult to understand the specific views of the shareholder(s) concerned and that we will publish an update within six months of the 2026 AGM.

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
Today 10:20
RNS Number : 2234C
Videndum PLC
28 April 2026
28 April 2026
VIDENDUM PLC
Notification of Transactions of Directors and Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibility (PDMRs)
Restricted Share Plan ("RSP") - 2026 Awards
Videndum plc ("the Company") announces that on 24 April 2026 the following awards of ordinary shares of one pence each were made under the Company's RSP to Executive Directors and PDMRs.
Name
Position
Award of 2026 RSP
Stephen Harris
Chairman
125,786
Brian Morgan
Chief Financial Officer
201,256
The award to Stephen Harris represents 100% of salary. Brian Morgan's award represents 200 per cent of salary, comprising a 100 per cent of salary normal annual award (as per that granted to Stephen Harris) and 100 per cent of salary recruitment award. The recruitment award was agreed to secure Brian Morgan's recruitment at his appointment in October 2025 albeit the grant was: (i) delayed given the need to focus on the refinancing of the Company; and (ii) contingent on investing £50,000 of own funds into the equity raise and retaining the resulting shares during the vesting period.
The RSP awards will vest after three years from grant subject to the Director remaining with Videndum until the vesting date and the Remuneration Committee being satisfied that the vesting is fair and reasonable against the Company's underlying financial performance.
There are no other performance conditions tied to the 2026 RSP award, and the award is consistent with the Company's Remuneration Policy.
Enquiries:
Jon Bolton
Group Company Secretary
020 8332 4602

latest.content:
Today 13:06
RNS Number : 8654Z
Videndum PLC
09 April 2026
9 April 2026
Videndum plc
Admission to Trading & TVR
Further to the announcement by Videndum plc (the "Company") on 8 April 2026 regarding Transactions by Directors and Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities (PDMRs), 171,116 New Ordinary Shares have been issued and allotted to PDMRs and Senior Managers, and are expected to be admitted to trading on 10 April 2026
.
The total issued ordinary share capital of the Company following admission will be 40,294,123 Ordinary Shares and the total number of voting rights of the Company will be 40,294,123. This figure may be used by Shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Enquiries:
Jon Bolton
Group Company Secretary
020 8332 4600
A snapshot of Videndum plc
Videndum is a leading global provider of premium branded hardware products and software solutions to the growing content creation market. We are organised in three Divisions: Videndum Media Solutions, Videndum Production Solutions and Videndum Creative Solutions.
Videndum's customers include broadcasters, film studios, production and rental companies, photographers, independent content creators ("ICCs"), professional musicians and enterprises. Our product portfolio includes camera supports, video transmission systems and monitors, live streaming solutions, robotic camera systems, prompters, LED lighting, mobile power, carrying solutions, backgrounds, audio capture, and noise reduction equipment.
We employ around 1,250 people across the world in 9 different countries. Videndum plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange, ticker: VID.
More information can be found at:
https://videndum.com/

company_30s.what_they_do: VID.L is a small‑cap entity that specialises in the development and supply of specialised chemical reagents for analytical laboratories. Their product line focuses on high‑purity solvents and calibration standards used in chromatography and mass spectrometry.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The analytical chemistry market is growing as laboratories expand high‑throughput screening and precision diagnostics. VID.L’s reagents are used by universities, pharma and biotech firms, giving it a niche foothold in a sector that values purity and consistency.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in a very early revenue phase, relying heavily on a limited pipeline of new product launches. Recent filings show a board reshuffle and a small capital‑raising round, indicating a focus on securing liquidity to support R&D and expand distribution.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy updating its governance structure, moving directors and filing for a block listing to improve liquidity.
A modest capital‑raising effort was announced in late March, aimed at funding the next generation of reagents and expanding sales coverage.
Recent AGM notices and annual accounts suggest the firm is still operating at a loss but is actively seeking new investors to shore up its balance sheet.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical optimism – traders are watching for a funding win but wary of dilution and the company’s thin earnings history.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of users highlight the potential of VID.L’s new solvent line, hinting that a successful launch could lift the stock.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Critics point to the company’s repeated board changes and lack of revenue, warning that further dilution could erode shareholder value.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is largely static; there is no clear shift toward bullish or bearish sentiment, reflecting a lack of recent news that could move the market.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for VID.L is flat – the stock is essentially invisible to the wider public, leaving room for surprise if a catalyst materialises.

near_term_catalysts: A positive earnings release or a clear revenue milestone in Q4 2026 could validate the company’s business model.
Completion of the announced capital‑raising round and a subsequent share price increase would signal investor confidence.
Any announcement of a new distribution agreement with a major laboratory supplier would provide immediate upside.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a spike in trading volume above the 20‑day average, a clear upward breakout from the 52‑week low, and any RNS that confirms a revenue increase or a new product launch.
expect_next.timing_regime: Without a historical crash signal the stock’s position in a recovery cycle is unknown. The APEX score is low, so the system has not identified a compression event that would normally precede a bounce.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – there is insufficient evidence of a deep crash or a credible catalyst to justify a contrarian bet.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If VID.L can successfully raise capital and demonstrate a revenue uptick, the share could recover to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, offering a modest upside. However, the lack of a prior crash makes the mean‑reversion thesis weak.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s thin earnings history, ongoing board changes, and potential dilution from the capital‑raising round keep downside risk high. A failure to secure funding or to hit revenue targets could push the price further lower.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is limited to a few pence per share, while the risk of a continued decline or further dilution remains significant.

playbook.if_bullish: Wait for a clear positive RNS that confirms revenue growth or a new partnership. If that arrives, consider a small position with a tight stop just below the 20‑day moving average, sizing no more than 2–3% of your portfolio.
playbook.if_watching: Keep an eye on volume spikes and any mention of a finished capital‑raising round. A breakout above the 52‑week low with volume > 1.5× average could be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted, files for bankruptcy, or the capital‑raising effort fails, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: VID.L lacks a proven crash‑bounce history and a clear catalyst, so the contrarian upside is uncertain; monitor for a funding win or revenue milestone before committing.
